title: caltha polypetala var. orthorhyncha
intro: Caltha polypetala var. orthorhyncha, also known as Marsh Marigold, is a flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of glycosides and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Marsh Marigold include its potential to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it can also be used in poultices and compresses to treat skin conditions and wounds.
